mnemonics.title: Euler Graph Decision
mnemonics.content: Even vertices = Euler Circuit | Two odds = Euler Path | Otherwise = No go!

mnemonics.title: Fleury's Rule
mnemonics.content: Never cross a bridge unless you have no other choice (bridge = danger!)

mnemonics.title: Euler's Theorem
mnemonics.content: All Even = Circuit | Two Odd = Path | Zero/Two only - that's the math!

mnemonics.title: Euler Path Start-End
mnemonics.content: Odd1 → Odd2 (start at one odd, end at the other)

mnemonics.title: Seven Bridges Origin
mnemonics.content: Königsberg had 7 bridges, Euler said NO to all 7!

acronyms.expansion: Eulerian - has Euler circuit (all even degrees)
Semi-Eulerian - has Euler path but not circuit (2 odd degrees)
F ind L east E xpens ive R emove Y our Bridges

visualTips.visualization: Draw two circles: one with all even dots (0 odd), one with exactly 2 odd dots. Think of odds as 'start/end points'.
Imagine walking on a tightrope over water - avoid it unless no other path exists!
Draw 4 land masses (A,B,C,D) with 7 lines (bridges) connecting them - check all have odd connections.
Think of weaving threads: make small circles, then weave them together at intersection points.

analogies.analogy: Like a mail carrier walking every street exactly once to deliver mail - that's a route using every edge!
A garbage truck collecting from every street and returning to start - all edges, back to origin!
A bridge is like the only bridge to an island - don't cross it unless forced, or you'll get stuck!
Even degree vertices are like rooms with even doors - you can enter and leave through different doors, always balancing out!

analogies.concept: Odd degree vertices in Euler path
analogies.analogy: Odd degree vertices are like entrance/exit points - start at one, end at the other!
